description_long: This is a large, sleek, silver-gray military jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply swept-back wings. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and feature prominent red star insignias near the tips, indicating a likely Soviet or Russian origin. The aircraft has twin jet engines mounted at the rear, with large exhaust nozzles visible from the back. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both swept back to match the wing design. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, with a clear canopy and a slightly raised profile for visibility. The overall design emphasizes speed and aerodynamic efficiency, typical of supersonic bombers or interceptors.
description: A large, silver-gray military jet with swept-back low wings, twin rear jet engines, and red star insignias. The streamlined fuselage and forward cockpit suggest a high-speed, long-range aircraft.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the sharply swept low wings, red star insignias, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated nose, forward cockpit, and the low-mounted swept wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin jet engine exhausts, swept tail surfaces, and the overall width of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the long fuselage, cockpit placement, and rear engine configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept wings with moderate anhedral angle and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines, rear-mounted on either side of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and swept horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single clear canopy, likely multi-seat.
